title: Select English Works of John Wyclif, Volume II
summary: The checked-in CME00030 XML is a complete University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse encoding of Thomas Arnold's 1871 volume II of Select English Works of John Wyclif: editorial preliminaries, 116 ferial Gospel sermons, 55 Sunday Epistle sermons, Vae Octuplex, and Of Mynystris in þe Chirche. Arnold founded the text on Bodleian MS Bodley 788 (A), supplied punctuation, capitalization, paragraphing, marginal analyses, biblical references, and sermon numbers, and cited selected readings from Douce 321 (E), Royal 18 B IX (G), St John's C.8 (H), Cotton Claudius D VIII (I), New College 95 (Q), and Laud Misc. 314 (C, for Vae Octuplex). Modern scholarship treats the 294-sermon cycle as unified but its single or multiple authorship as unresolved; the two tracts are related pendants, not demonstrated translations of Wyclif's Latin expositions. Complete scans of Arnold volumes I and II, complete 370-canvas and 180-canvas Trinity facsimiles, and the complete ten-canvas Bodleian public selection are cached. The Bodleian selection has no CME00030 overlap, and complete public surrogates were not found for the base witness or six manuscripts represented in Arnold's selected apparatus, so dated negatives and mediated reproduction routes are recorded.
review_notes: The edition-defined aggregate, sermon-cycle components, associated tracts, manuscript witnesses, Arnold edition, modern facsimiles, U-M encoding, and repository XML remain distinct layers.
Arnold's confident John Wyclif attribution is preserved as historical evidence but not promoted over modern scholarship on anonymous and possibly collaborative composition.

Only manuscripts whose readings are cited in CME00030 are called collated sources; Trinity B.2.17 (K) and B.4.20 (M) are recorded as consulted or described by Arnold, not silently upgraded to collated sources.
Complete-facsimile negatives are limited to public web access checked on 2026-07-12 and do not imply that onsite access, repository photography, or microfilm is impossible.
The Trinity bundles are complete relative to the provider-exposed canvas sequences and contain covers, endleaves, blanks, all leaves, and adjacent texts, but no separate spine or edge views.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00030
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The file is 1,140,095 bytes at the recorded CME submodule commit.
TEMPHEAD records review by lattaj for MURP on 27 May 2016; this is not an initial digitization date.
The complete file has 15 DIV1, 175 DIV2, 2 DIV3, 356 HEAD, 638 P, 2 LG, 13 L, 1,387 NOTE, and 442 PB elements.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME digital text CME00030
entities.description: A public structured digital text representing Arnold's complete 1871 volume II, including edition title page, notices, contents, prefaces, page milestones, headings, notes, and the four medieval content sections.

date_statements.label: documented XML review
date_statements.notes: The TEMPHEAD date records review activity, not initial encoding.

notes: TEXT/@LANG is enm, appropriate to the medieval prose; the edition apparatus is modern English and biblical lemmata include Latin.
The encoding follows Arnold's printed pagination through p. 423 and includes all four medieval content sections rather than an excerpt.

entities.label: Thomas Arnold's 1871 Select English Works of John Wyclif, volume II
entities.description: The immediate source edition for CME00030: a complete edited volume of ferial Gospel sermons, Sunday Epistle sermons, Vae Octuplex, and Of Mynystris in þe Chirche, with editorial preliminaries and apparatus.

bibliographic.citation: Select English Works of John Wyclif, ed. Thomas Arnold, vol. II, Sermons on the Ferial Gospels and Sunday Epistles; Treatises (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1871).
bibliographic.title: Select English Works of John Wyclif

notes: Arnold calls Bodley 788 the foundation of his text and says he nearly reproduces its orthography while retaining thorn and yogh, but substitutes v for manuscript u and j for manuscript i where sound requires.
He supplied capitals, paragraphs, punctuation, marginal analyses, biblical references, and verse or sermon numbers because the base manuscript lacked those reading aids.
The printed order keeps the sermon divisions together and relocates Vae Octuplex and Of Mynystris from their manuscript positions after the Sunday Gospel and Proprium Sanctorum sections.
Modern scholarship warns that Arnold examined most manuscripts other than Bodley 788 only cursorily.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Arnold's 1871 volume II
entities.description: A complete public 454-page PDF of the bound Princeton Theological Seminary Library copy, including covers, preliminary leaves, all numbered pages, final leaves, and photographed blanks or targets exposed by the provider.
entities.reproduction_format: 454-page color PDF; 342 x 596 point pages in the cached derivative
notes: The item metadata incorrectly gives 1869 for the multi-volume catalog record; the photographed volume-II title page establishes 1871.
No separately exposed spine or edge views appear in the cached PDF.

entities.label: Thomas Arnold's 1869 Select English Works of John Wyclif, volume I
entities.description: The first volume of Arnold's edition, containing his general introduction, manuscript account, editorial policy, Sunday Gospel sermons, and Commune and Proprium Sanctorum sermons. It supports the source-lineage account for volume II but does not contain CME00030's medieval components.

bibliographic.citation: Select English Works of John Wyclif, ed. Thomas Arnold, vol. I, Sermons on the Gospels for Sundays and Festivals (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1869).
bibliographic.title: Select English Works of John Wyclif

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Arnold's 1869 volume I
entities.description: A complete public 458-page PDF of the bound Princeton Theological Seminary Library copy used here for Arnold's introduction and editorial method.
entities.reproduction_format: 458-page color PDF; 359 x 594 point pages in the cached derivative

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Bodley 788
entities.description: A late-fourteenth-century English parchment codex containing a calendar, the Wycliffite sermon cycle, and an associated treatise; Arnold adopted it as the complete foundation of his edition.

holding.notes: Arnold's siglum A; current MARCO record links a ten-image 35mm-slide selection.

notes: The current catalog describes illuminated initials; a complete current digital surrogate is needed for whole-object verification.
Arnold says it is complete and accurate and makes it the foundation of his printed text.
Hudson notes that Arnold printed this manuscript while treating most others less fully.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selection for MS Bodley 788
entities.description: The complete provider-exposed selection comprises ten canvases from 35mm slides, ff. 1r-5v. These leaves belong to Sunday Gospel material in Arnold's volume I and do not overlap CME00030.
entities.reproduction_format: 10-canvas IIIF Presentation object from 35mm slides; cached as ten JPEG derivatives constrained to 1800 pixels
notes: The provider selection is complete, but the represented manuscript coverage is partial and excludes all CME00030 content, bindings, most leaves, and separate spine or edge views.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Douce 321
entities.description: An early-fifteenth-century parchment codex of Wycliffite sermons, now substantially imperfect, with Sunday materials and ferial Gospel sermons but without the two associated tracts.

holding.notes: Arnold's siglum E; current catalog states that the manuscript is not available online.

notes: Current catalog evidence places ferial Gospel sermons at ff. 167-254, followed by a table at f. 255; relevant Sunday material survives earlier with major lacunae.
Arnold calls it a good but mutilated witness and cites readings under E; his volume-II preface says it lacks Vae Octuplex and Of Mynystris.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Laud Misc. 314
entities.description: A fifteenth-century parchment codex containing Sunday Gospel sermons and Vae Octuplex; Arnold cites its readings as C in the tract.

holding.notes: Arnold's siglum C; current catalog states that the manuscript is not available online.

notes: The current record describes a fifteenth-century binding.
The exact modern folio locus of Vae Octuplex was not established from the public catalog extract.

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 18 B IX
entities.description: An early-fifteenth-century English vellum codex containing interleaved Sunday and ferial sermons, Of Mynystris, another tract, Vae Octuplex, and an omitted Epistle homily; Arnold cites selected readings as G.

holding.notes: Arnold's siglum G; no digitized content is exposed in the current catalog record.

notes: The catalog places interleaved homilies at ff. 1-177, Of Mynystris at ff. 177-185, Vae Octuplex at ff. 191v-194v, and an omitted Epistle homily at ff. 194v-196.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Claudius D VIII
entities.description: A composite English manuscript whose mid-fifteenth-century second part, ff. 109-310, is an imperfect Middle English commentary or Wycliffite sermon collection; Arnold cites selected readings as I.

holding.notes: Arnold's siglum I; no digitized content is exposed in the current parent or component record.

physical_description.extent: sermon component ff. 109-310 within a composite codex

notes: Exact subranges for the CME00030 sermon groups remain unresolved because the current component description does not map each sermon division.

entities.label: St John's College Cambridge MS C.8
entities.description: An imperfect fifteenth-century witness of the Wycliffite sermon cycle. Arnold says at least fifty-five sermons are missing and cites selected readings as H.

holding.notes: A current exact item-level public catalog URL and complete online surrogate were not found on 2026-07-12.

entities.label: New College Oxford MS 95
entities.description: A fifteenth-century manuscript containing a complete set of ferial Gospel sermons, seven Proprium sermons, and one Sunday sermon; Arnold cites selected ferial readings as Q.

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.value: fifteenth century, before 1500; modern bibliography also suggests circa 1425

holding.notes: A current exact item-level public catalog URL and complete online surrogate were not found on 2026-07-12.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS B.2.17
entities.description: A fifteenth-century parchment codex containing Sunday Gospel, Commune, and Proprium sermons, Of Mynystris, and ferial Gospel sermons. Arnold describes and examined it as K but CME00030 does not cite K readings.

holding.notes: Arnold's siglum K; the catalog exposes a complete 370-canvas IIIF object.

physical_description.format: double-column bound manuscript codex with stamped leather binding over boards
notes: The catalog gives 43 lines per column.
CME00030 overlap is Of Mynystris ff. 112v-121v and ferial Gospel sermons ff. 122v-180v; the codex lacks Sunday Epistle sermons and Vae Octuplex.

entities.label: Complete Trinity College Cambridge IIIF facsimile of MS B.2.17
entities.description: The complete provider-exposed 370-canvas physical-object sequence, from front cover through back cover, including pastedowns or endleaves, blanks, all manuscript leaves, adjacent sermon sections, and rear end matter.
entities.reproduction_format: 370-canvas IIIF Presentation object; cached as JPEG derivatives constrained to 1800 pixels
notes: No separate spine or edge views are exposed.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS B.4.20
entities.description: An imperfect late-fourteenth- or early-fifteenth-century parchment codex containing extant Sunday Epistle and Sunday Gospel sermons, Vae Octuplex, Commune and Proprium sermons, and the opening of Of Mynystris. Arnold describes it as M but CME00030 does not cite M readings.

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.value: late fourteenth or early fifteenth century

holding.notes: Arnold's siglum M; the catalog exposes a complete 180-canvas IIIF object.

physical_description.format: bound manuscript codex in two hands, physically imperfect through lost quires
notes: The catalog records 64 and 51 lines per page and multiple missing leaves or quires.
Direct image inspection maps extant Sunday Epistle sermons to ff. 1r-14v, Vae Octuplex to ff. 47v-50r, and the surviving opening of Of Mynystris to f. 80v; ferial Gospel sermons are absent.
The Sunday Epistle section is imperfect at its beginning, and the Of Mynystris text loses its continuation after f. 80v.

entities.label: Complete Trinity College Cambridge IIIF facsimile of MS B.4.20
entities.description: The complete provider-exposed 180-canvas sequence, including both covers, inside boards, five front flyleaves, all eighty manuscript folios, rear flyleaves and stub, and adjacent texts.
entities.reproduction_format: 180-canvas IIIF Presentation object; cached as JPEG derivatives constrained to 1800 pixels
notes: No separate spine or edge views are exposed.

rights.basis: The U-M item applies the No Copyright - United States rights statement to the digital text.
notes: This is a United States rights statement, not a worldwide public-domain determination.

rights.component: printed edition text and typographical content
rights.basis: The edition was published in Oxford in 1871; current public providers identify the text or file as public domain.
The Wikimedia Commons record for the Internet Archive-derived scan applies the Public Domain Mark and identifies the work as public domain in its country of origin and the United States.
notes: The exact Internet Archive metadata contains no rights or license field; the conclusion relies on the historical publication and the Commons record for the IA-derived scan.

rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The underlying 1869 edition is public domain in the United States, but the exact Internet Archive metadata record exposes no item-specific rights or license field for the scan.
notes: Public download and the age of the printed source do not by themselves establish a reuse license for the digital-image files.

rights.component: IMEP summary-list PDF and its modern catalog text
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The exact PDF is publicly downloadable, but no item-specific license or reuse statement was identified in the document or download record.
notes: Public access does not by itself grant permission to redistribute the modern catalog text.

rights.basis: The IIIF manifest and Digital Bodleian terms attribute the photographs to the Bodleian Libraries and apply CC BY-NC 4.0 to the exposed images.
notes: The license governs the modern images, not the medieval text; commercial use requires separate permission.

rights.basis: The catalog and IIIF manifest apply CC BY-NC 4.0 and identify the Master and Fellows of Trinity College as image rightsholder.
The catalog and IIIF manifest apply CC BY-NC 4.0 and identify the Master and Fellows of Trinity College as image rightsholder.

editorial_practices.description: Arnold supplied punctuation because Bodley 788 presented the text without punctuation.
editorial_practices.scope: All edited medieval texts in the volume

editorial_practices.description: Arnold supplied capitals and paragraph divisions absent from the base manuscript.
editorial_practices.scope: All edited medieval texts in the volume
editorial_practices.source_feature: Base-manuscript text without systematic capitals or paragraphing

editorial_practices.description: Arnold separated the sermon categories into print volumes and moved Vae Octuplex and Of Mynystris from their positions in Bodley 788 so that the sermon sequences would remain unbroken.
editorial_practices.scope: Volume organization and placement of the two tracts
editorial_practices.source_feature: In Bodley 788, Vae follows the Sunday Gospel sermons and Of Mynystris follows Proprium Sanctorum
editorial_practices.representation: Both tracts printed after the ferial Gospel and Sunday Epistle sections in volume II

editorial_practices.description: Arnold printed selected variants from other manuscripts while leaving many unreported readings and errors silent; the XML preserves notes naming E, G, H, I, Q, and C.
editorial_practices.scope: Selected passages in sermons and Vae Octuplex
editorial_practices.source_feature: Variant readings in manuscripts other than Bodley 788
editorial_practices.representation: Footnotes or notes keyed by manuscript sigla
notes: This is a selective apparatus, not a complete modern critical collation.

editorial_practices.description: The encoding represents printed titles, sermon headings, marginal summaries, biblical text emphasis, notes, and page boundaries with structural elements and rendition attributes inherited from Arnold's edition.
editorial_practices.source_feature: Arnold's typography, headings, marginalia, notes, and printed pagination
editorial_practices.representation: DIV, HEAD, NOTE, HI, PB, Q, and related XML elements

evidence.summary: Confirms complete Arnold volume-II coverage, 116 ferial Gospel sermons, 55 Sunday Epistle sermons, two prose tracts, 442 page milestones, and that all 13 encoded verse lines occur in modern editorial quotations rather than medieval verse units.

evidence.summary: Current official evidence for shelfmark, date, 180-folio parchment codex, dimensions, binding, contents and loci, complete IIIF link, and CC BY-NC 4.0 image terms.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, MS B.2.17 IIIF Presentation manifest.
evidence.locator: all 370 canvases; CME00030 parts canvases 230-248 and 250-366
evidence.summary: Machine-readable evidence for complete exposed object coverage, canvas labels and order, image services, Trinity attribution, and CC BY-NC 4.0 license.

evidence.citation: Inspection of the complete cached Trinity College Cambridge IIIF bundle for MS B.2.17.
evidence.locator: all 370 canvases; Of Mynystris canvases 230-248; ferial Gospel sermons canvases 250-366
evidence.summary: Confirms the complete provider sequence and the two discontiguous CME00030-related runs, with adjacent contents and object boundaries preserved.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, James Catalogue of Western Manuscripts, MS B.4.20 (James 134).
evidence.locator: whole 80-folio codex; Sunday Epistle ending f. 14v; Sunday Gospel section from f. 16; final Matthew 24 exposition at f. 80v
evidence.summary: Current official evidence for shelfmark, late-fourteenth- or early-fifteenth-century date, parchment support, dimensions, hands, physical lacunae, contents, complete IIIF link, and CC BY-NC 4.0 image terms.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, MS B.4.20 IIIF Presentation manifest.
evidence.locator: all 180 canvases; manuscript folios at canvases 13-172
evidence.summary: Machine-readable evidence for complete exposed object coverage, canvas labels and order, image services, Trinity attribution, and CC BY-NC 4.0 license.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection of the complete Trinity IIIF image sequence for MS B.4.20.
evidence.locator: all 180 canvases; extant Sunday Epistles canvases 13-40; Vae Octuplex canvases 106-111; Of Mynystris opening canvas 172
evidence.summary: Confirms complete provider-exposed physical coverage and exact CME00030 mappings: Sunday Epistles ff. 1r-14v, Vae Octuplex ff. 47v-50r, and the surviving Of Mynystris opening at f. 80v; it also confirms the manuscript's missing beginning, missing ferial sermons, and lost end.

evidence.citation: Index of Middle English Prose, Summary Lists of Middle English Prose Contents, vol. 11, Trinity College Cambridge, entries for B.2.17 and B.4.20.
evidence.summary: Current official summary evidence distinguishing the sermon groups, Vae Octuplex, and Of Mynystris as separate contents in the two Trinity codices.

evidence.citation: Wikimedia Commons, File: Select English works of John Wyclif (IA selectenglishworks02wycl).pdf.
evidence.summary: Public provider record identifying the Internet Archive source and Princeton Theological Seminary copy, 454-page extent, and Public Domain Mark for the work in its country of origin and the United States.

evidence.citation: Online Library of Liberty, Select English Works of John Wyclif, vol. II.
evidence.summary: Independent public facsimile and bibliographic record identifying the 1871 volume and stating that the text is in the public domain.

open_questions.question: Can complete current color facsimiles of Bodley 788, Douce 321, and Laud Misc. 314 be obtained to verify Arnold's base and Bodleian collations directly?
next_steps: Request whole-object photography for each manuscript through Bodleian Imaging Services.
Specify covers, boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, all leaves, and edges where offered; separately map all CME00030 component ranges.
For Bodley 788, reconcile exact modern folio ranges with the Hudson-Gradon edition before selective comparison.
For Douce 321, compare any available mediated microfilm but do not treat it as a public complete color facsimile.

open_questions.question: Can complete color facsimiles of Royal MS 18 B IX and Cotton MS Claudius D VIII be obtained to verify G and I readings, codex structure, and exact CME00030 ranges?
next_steps: Request complete-object photography through British Library Digitisation Services for both shelfmarks.
Include every cover, spine, pastedown, flyleaf, blank, leaf, and edge where offered; map the cataloged component ranges separately.
Record supplied-file rights and terms before caching, then collate only the readings actually relevant to Arnold's apparatus.

open_questions.question: What are the current item-level catalog records, exact CME00030 folio ranges, access conditions, and complete reproduction routes for St John's College Cambridge MS C.8 and New College Oxford MS 95?
next_steps: Ask each college to confirm current shelfmark, extent, date, content mapping, and any existing microfilm or photography.
Request complete-object images with bindings, endleaves, blanks, all leaves, and edges where offered.
Do not infer exact loci from Arnold's nineteenth-century descriptions or the later witness list alone.

open_questions.question: Who first keyed or converted CME00030, when was it first published, and what quality-control transformations preceded the documented 2016 review?
next_steps: Consult U-M collection documentation, archived item metadata, and project records for initial conversion and publication provenance.
Do not treat the TEMPHEAD review date as the creation date.
Compare the public delivery text and checked-in XML if a stable machine-readable U-M export becomes available.

open_questions.question: What authorship, revision, and manuscript relationship model should replace Arnold's Wyclif attribution and base-plus-selected-variants presentation for the sermon cycle and two tracts?
next_steps: Use the complete Hudson-Gradon edition and subsequent scholarship for current textual states, witness groupings, and authorship arguments.
Keep the unified cycle, its possible collaboration, the two related tracts, and their Latin analogues analytically distinct.
Collate direct witness images where available rather than reconstructing a modern stemma from Arnold's sparse apparatus.
